text: Earth. After the ten preceding threats, the prophet denounces destruction to the whole world, (Worthington) at the day of judgment; though he may also allude to the desolation of the promised land, as our Saviour joins both in the same prediction, Matthew xxiv. (Calmet)
Priest. All distinctions shall be disregarded. (Worthington)<hr>When Jerusalem was taken, all became captives.
Weakened: Joakim, etc., are made prisoners. The greatest monarchs must come before God's tribunal.

Sin. Towards the end of the world iniquity will abound, and men shall rage against each other, Matthew xxiv. (Worthington)<hr>They will also feel the effects of sin.<hr>Mad: abandoned to their passions, (Deuteronomy 28:28.) excepting only the elect. (Menochius)<hr>Few. The Chaldeans permitted only a few of the poorest sort to remain, 2 Paralipomenon 29:10.
Vanity. Jerusalem, (Calmet) or any other city, will be all in confusion. (Haydock)<hr>In, as was the case in times of mourning, Jeremias 9:21. (Calmet)
Sea. The few elect (ver. 13.) being rescued from the misery of the world, shall praise God. (Haydock)<hr>They are exhorted to lift up their heads, Luke 21:28. (Menochius)

Instruction. The Church is like an island, compared with the rest of the world; or it preaches the gospel to all nations, and to the islands, like Great Britain. (Worthington)<hr>Apostolic men are required to preach incessantly to all sorts of people. Hebrew, "in light;" or Pagnin, "in vales." (Menochius)
I said. The prophet, or any other, may speak thus in the latter days. (Calmet)<hr>Myself. I cannot recount what horrid pains I beheld. (St. Jerome) (Menochius) (2 Corinthians 12:4.)
Snare. He alludes to the methods of taking wild beasts, Job 18:11.<hr>Opened, as they were in the days of Noe[Noah]. (Calmet)<hr>All sorts of misery hang over us.
Night, unexpectedly, (Haydock) and with the utmost speed. (Calmet)

High. The stars, which in many places of the Scripture are so called. Some commentators explain that these words here signify the demons of the air. (Challoner)<hr>The apostate angels will be judged, 1 Corinthians 6:3., and Matthew 24:29.

Visited. Hence Origen (Prin. 3:6., etc.) took occasion to assert, that the damned would one day be released, though the Scripture so often declares the contrary. The prophet speaks of the future liberation of the Jews; (Calmet) or he intimates that after many days, yea throughout eternity, the reprobate will still be punished. (Menochius)
Blush: be turned into blood, Joel 2:10. Dreadful calamities shall ensue, to usher in the great day of judgment. (Calmet)
